2604.05757.title: Identifying Influential N-grams in Confidence Calibration via Regression Analysis
2604.05757.paper_summary: While large language models (LLMs) improve performance by explicit reasoning, their responses are often overconfident, even though they include linguistic expressions demonstrating uncertainty. In this work, we identify what linguistic expressions are related to confidence by applying the regression method. Specifically, we predict confidence of those linguistic expressions in the reasoning parts of LLMs as the dependent variables and analyze the relationship between a specific $n$-gram and confidence. Across multiple models and QA benchmarks, we show that LLMs remain overconfident when reasoning is involved and attribute this behavior to specific linguistic information. Interestingly, several of the extracted expressions coincide with cue phrases intentionally inserted on test-time scaling to improve reasoning performance. Through our test on causality and verification that the extracted linguistic information truly affects confidence, we reveal that confidence calibration is possible by simply suppressing those overconfident expressions without drops in performance.
2604.05757.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）透過明確推理來提升性能，但它們的回應往往過於自信，即使它們包含表達不確定性的語言表達。在這項工作中，我們透過應用回歸方法來識別與自信相關的語言表達。具體而言，我們預測這些語言表達在LLMs推理部分的自信度作為因變量，並分析特定 $n$-gram 與自信之間的關係。在多個模型和問答基準中，我們顯示LLMs在涉及推理時仍然過於自信，並將這種行為歸因於特定的語言信息。有趣的是，幾個提取的表達與在測試時刻刻意插入的提示短語重合，以改善推理性能。通過我們對因果關係的測試和驗證，證明提取的語言信息確實影響自信，我們揭示僅僅通過抑制那些過於自信的表達而不降低性能，自信校準是可能的。
2604.05757.author: Shintaro Ozaki et.al.
2604.05757.authors: Shintaro Ozaki, Wataru Hashimoto, Hidetaka Kamigaito, Katsuhiko Hayashi, Taro Watanabe
